note: Includes 2,459 in-theater deaths plus 2 who died from wounds later. Does not include ~1,000+ who died from suicides after returning home.
Highest coalition death toll after the US. Majority killed in Helmand province during intense fighting 2006-2013.
Includes US, UK, Canada (158), France (90), Germany (57), Italy (53), and 45 other nations. Does not include local Afghan security force casualties.

source: Brown Univ. Costs of War Project 2021
note: Afghan government never released systematic casualty data. Includes ANDSF, Afghan National Police, and National Directorate of Security. Figure based on academic estimates from multiple sources.
UNAMA began systematic tracking in 2009. Pre-2009 civilian casualties are not systematically documented. Total since 2001 estimated at 70,000-100,000+ by Costs of War Project.

source: Pakistan ISPR / South Asia Terrorism Portal
note: Pakistan suffered heavily fighting TTP and al-Qaeda affiliate groups in FATA and KPK. Includes casualties from operations Zarb-e-Azb, Rah-e-Rast, and other major operations.

source: South Asia Terrorism Portal / Pakistan ISPR
note: Pakistan suffered massive civilian casualties from TTP, al-Qaeda, and affiliated groups. SATP records 70,000+ terrorist and counter-terrorism related deaths 2003-2024.

source: DoD Contractors in Afghanistan / Brown Univ. Costs of War
note: US private contractor deaths exceeded US military deaths. Contractors provided logistics, security, training, and maintenance. Deaths underreported and less publicly discussed.
No reliable aggregate data. ISAF and Afghan government statistics were inflated. Academic estimates range widely. Taliban never published own casualty data. Figure contested by all sides.
Combined US/NATO and Taliban operations killed thousands of ISIS-K fighters. MOAB alone claimed 94 killed; sustained Taliban counterterrorism campaign continued post-2021.
Afghanistan has been one of the world's deadliest countries for journalists. Attacks came from Taliban, ISIS-K, and unclear perpetrators. Targeted killings of journalists surged under Taliban rule.

source: UN OCHA / Aid Worker Security Database
note: Afghanistan ranks among world's most dangerous countries for aid workers. MSF hospital bombing (42 killed), targeted assassinations, and IEDs all killed humanitarian workers.
Deadliest single event for US forces in a decade. Most victims were Afghan civilians attempting evacuation. ISIS-K claimed responsibility; US conducted retaliatory strikes in Nangarhar.

category: Afghan Civilians Killed in Drone/Airstrike 'Signature' Strikes
note: Total civilians killed in US-led airstrikes remains one of the war's most contested figures. Pentagon systematically undercounted. Bureau of Investigative Journalism tracked 10,000+ Afghan civilian airstrike deaths.
Deadliest terrorist attack in Pakistan's history. TTP sent 6 gunmen who killed children at Peshawar military school in retaliation for military operations. Led to lifting of death penalty moratorium.
